scanDefaultsPage.performanceDescription: Control scan speed and resource usage
scanDefaultsPage.performanceTip: Higher concurrency scans faster but uses more resources. A timeout of 10s and concurrency of 20 is optimal for most audits.
scanDefaultsPage.modulesDescription: Enable or disable scan modules by default. Individual scans can override these.
scanDefaultsPage.portScanDescription: Scan 14 critical ports (SSH, RDP, SMB, MySQL, etc.) for exposure detection
scanDefaultsPage.webChecksDescription: HTTP headers, TLS/SSL, WAF detection, cookies, SRI, secrets, security.txt
scanDefaultsPage.dnsChecksDescription: DNSSEC, zone transfer (AXFR), SPF, DMARC, MX redundancy
scanDefaultsPage.whoisChecksDescription: Check domain registration expiry dates and warn before 30-day threshold
scanDefaultsPage.loadFailed: Could not load saved defaults — saving is disabled to avoid overwriting your existing settings.

apiKeysPage.name: Name
apiKeysPage.documentationDescription: Use your API key in the Authorization header as a Bearer token. See the API documentation for available endpoints and usage examples.
apiKeysPage.noKeysDescription: Create a key to authenticate CI/CD pipelines, monitoring integrations, or any external system that needs to call the API.

compliancePage.title: NIS2 Compliance Matrix
compliancePage.subtitle: Art. 24(2) D.Lgs 138/2024 (transposes Art. 21 NIS2) — Cybersecurity risk management measures
compliancePage.noDataDescription: Run a compliance scan to populate the Art. 21 matrix automatically. Automated checks cover TLS, DNS, HTTP security headers, encryption, and more.
compliancePage.infoBody: This matrix maps Art. 21(2) requirements of the NIS2 Directive (EU 2022/2555), transposed into Italian law as D.Lgs 138/2024. Automated checks verify TLS, DNS, HTTP security headers, encryption, and incident handling. "Manual Review" entries require attestation from the compliance team.

auditLogPage.title: Audit Log
auditLogPage.subtitle: Track all changes and actions in your organization
auditLogPage.activityDescription: Showing recent actions. Audit logs are retained for 90 days.
auditLogPage.noEntriesDescription: Audit entries appear here whenever a user creates, updates or deletes resources.

hero.headlineStart: NIS2 governance, technical validation, and incident response —
hero.subtitle: The open-source platform for the EU NIS2 Directive (2022/2555). Bridge the gap between Art. 21 policy and what your network actually does — without sending a single byte of scan data to a third party.

features.heading: Everything Art. 21 asks for, in one auditable platform.
features.subtitle: Most NIS2 work is human work. The platform automates the parts that should be automated, and tracks the parts that legally require a person to sign their name to.

governance.title: Governance Framework
governance.body: 30-item checklist mapped to NIS2 Art. 21 (a)–(j), with owner, evidence, and review-cadence tracking. Cross-referenced to the Italian D.Lgs 138/2024 transposition and ACN determine.

validation.title: Technical Validation
validation.body: 30+ async checks on TLS, DNS, certificates, HTTP headers, port exposure, secrets, and resilience. The probe that verifies if the policy your governance framework documents is actually enforced on the wire.

incident.title: Incident Response
incident.body: Art. 23 lifecycle with the 24h / 72h / 1-month deadlines tracked as live countdowns. The Red Button generates a CSIRT-ready Early Warning JSON from three fields plus the latest asset inventory.

supply.title: Supply Chain Risk
supply.body: Vendor inventory with 4-level criticality, security scoring, contract tracking (SLA, audit rights, security clauses), and ACN Art. 18 fields for Italian transposition.

bia.title: Business Impact Analysis
bia.body: Process inventory with RTO / RPO / MTPD, five-dimension impact scoring, asset and vendor dependency mapping, and automatic BCP / DRP gap detection.

ai.title: AI Copilot + MCP
ai.body: Optional remediation copilot via Ollama (air-gapped) or OpenAI. Native Model Context Protocol server lets Claude, Cursor, and other AI agents query your compliance posture directly.

showcase.heading: Posture you can read in 30 seconds.
showcase.subtitle: One dashboard for total scans, average compliance score, open findings, and monitored assets — with the recent-scan trend on the same screen.
showcase.screenshotAlt: NIS2 Platform dashboard: total scans, compliance score, findings, monitored assets, and the welcome onboarding panel

how.heading: From clone to first executive report in an afternoon.

step1.title: Clone and run locally
step1.body: make dev — the platform comes up on http://localhost:8077 with Postgres, Redis, and the API in one compose file.

step2.title: Map your governance
step2.body: Walk the 30-item Art. 21 checklist with owners and evidence. Add assets, vendors, and processes. Generate the executive PDF for the board.

step3.title: Run the technical probe
step3.body: Schedule scans against your domains and IP ranges. Findings flow into the same workspace as the governance posture. Cross-reference each finding to the Art. 21 sub-paragraph it weakens.

audiences.heading: Built with the people who actually use it.
audiences.subtitle: One platform that speaks fluently to the boardroom and to the SOC.

ciso.body: Bridge the gap between the policy you signed off and what the network actually does. Boardroom-ready evidence on a deadline.

dpo.body: GDPR / ePrivacy posture surfaced separately from NIS2 — never aggregated into the wrong score. Vendor risk and incident workflows aligned to your obligations.

consultant.body: Multi-tenant by design. Manage every client in one self-hosted instance. White-label PDF reports per organisation; switch tenants without logging out.

secops.body: 30+ async checks shipped, MCP-ready for AI agents, Prometheus-friendly metrics, scheduled scans via cron. No third-party SaaS in your tenant blast radius.

selfHosted.heading: Your scan data never leaves your infrastructure.
selfHosted.subtitle: A CISO of an essential entity will not upload their vulnerability data to a third-party SaaS. So we built the platform around the assumption that it won't.
selfHosted.point1: Your PostgreSQL, your data — no telemetry, no external calls, no cloud dependencies.
selfHosted.point2: Air-gapped support: Ollama AI copilot runs entirely local. OpenAI is opt-in.
selfHosted.point3: Postgres FORCE ROW LEVEL SECURITY enforces tenant isolation in the database, not just the app.
selfHosted.point4: AGPL-3.0 — own your fork forever. Commercial dual-licensing available.
selfHosted.production: Production: <cmd>make prod</cmd> — Caddy auto-HTTPS, all services healthy-gated.

finalCta.heading: Stop talking about NIS2. Start showing the matrix.
finalCta.subtitle: Self-host the platform in 60 seconds, or open the dashboard now if you already have an account. AGPL-3.0 — yours, forever.
